prompt:
Here is my AG configuration output. Please review it with /sqlag-review:

--- Query 1: Instance and AG Overview ---
hadr_enabled  product_version  edition                        ag_name   failure_condition_level  health_check_timeout  automated_backup_preference_desc  db_failover  basic_features  is_contained  required_synchronized_secondaries_to_commit
1             16.0.4175.1      Enterprise Edition (64-bit)    ProdAG    1                        30000                 SECONDARY                         0            0               0             0

--- Query 2: Replica Configuration ---
ag_name  replica_server_name  availability_mode_desc  failover_mode_desc  session_timeout  primary_role_allow_connections_desc  secondary_role_allow_connections_desc  backup_priority  seeding_mode_desc  endpoint_url                   read_only_routing_url  join_state_desc
ProdAG   PROD-SQL01           SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT      AUTOMATIC           10               ALL                                  ALL                                    50               MANUAL             TCP://PROD-SQL01:5022          NULL                   JOINED
ProdAG   PROD-SQL02           SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT      AUTOMATIC           10               ALL                                  ALL                                    50               MANUAL             TCP://PROD-SQL02:5022          NULL                   JOINED
ProdAG   PROD-SQL03           ASYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT     MANUAL              10               ALL                                  NO                                     50               MANUAL             TCP://DR-SQL01:5022            NULL                   JOINED

--- Query 3: Listener and IP Configuration ---
ag_name  dns_name    port  ip_address    ip_subnet_mask  ip_state  is_conformant
ProdAG   prod-ag-l   1433  10.0.1.100    255.255.255.0   ONLINE    1

--- Query 4: Mirroring Endpoint ---
name           state_desc  role_desc  connection_auth_desc     encryption_algorithm_desc  encryption_desc  port
Hadr_endpoint  STARTED     ALL        WINDOWS NEGOTIATE         AES                        SUPPORTED        5022

--- Query 5: AG Database Recovery Models ---
ag_database_id  database_name  recovery_model_desc  is_read_committed_snapshot_on  state_desc
{abc-guid}      SalesDB        FULL                 0                              ONLINE
{def-guid}      ReportDB       SIMPLE               0                              ONLINE
expected_behavior: Should fire: F5 (failure_condition_level=1, too permissive), F8 (session_timeout=10 on ASYNC DR-SQL01), F4 (endpoint encryption=SUPPORTED, not REQUIRED), F2 (ReportDB in SIMPLE recovery), F15 (no read_only_routing_url despite ALL connections on PROD-SQL01/02), F10 (all secondaries at backup_priority=50 with SECONDARY preference). Should not fire F1 (hadr_enabled=1), F3 (endpoint STARTED), F11 (all JOINED).

prompt: We have a two-node Basic Availability Group on SQL Server 2019 Standard Edition. Both nodes are PROD-SQL01 (primary) and PROD-SQL02 (secondary). We have three databases in the AG: OrdersDB, CustomersDB, and ArchiveDB. The secondary is not configured as readable. We're using automatic seeding and Windows authentication on the endpoint. The listener is on port 1433. We'd like to offload reporting to the secondary. Run /sqlag-review.
expected_behavior: Should fire: F29 (Basic AG with 3 databases, limit is 1), F30 (Basic AG readable secondary not supported — user wants to offload reporting but this isn't possible on Basic AG). Should inform user that readable secondary is unavailable on Basic AG regardless of allow_connections setting, and that they must reduce to 1 database per AG or upgrade to Enterprise Edition. Should not assume runtime health issues since no DMV data provided — should note which checks are NOT ASSESSED due to missing input.

prompt: I'm setting up a distributed availability group between our on-premises datacenter (PrimaryAG with listener primary-ag-l.corp.local:5022) and our DR site (SecondaryAG with listener dr-ag-l.corp.local:5022). The CREATE AVAILABILITY GROUP script I used pointed the AVAILABILITY GROUP ON to TCP://DR-SQL01\MSSQLSERVER:5022 instead of the listener URL. Also, the inter-site link is set to SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT because we want zero data loss to DR. Review this with /sqlag-review.
expected_behavior: Should fire: F28 (distributed AG using instance name TCP://DR-SQL01\MSSQLSERVER:5022 instead of listener URL TCP://dr-ag-l.corp.local:5022) and F32 (distributed AG link set to SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT, which is not enforced — link always behaves asynchronously, so RPO=0 to DR cannot be achieved this way). Should explain that zero data loss across a distributed AG is architecturally impossible and suggest alternative approaches (stretch cluster, FCI at DR site).
